content.full_text:
FPPC

JUN 19 851 M'90

5787 Highwood Road
Castro Valley, CA 94552
June 15, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallibrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Committee
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Hallibrin:

Susan Barbrow from your office suggested I write to you about a concern I have. It is about the fact that during a Board retreat which included members of the board, administration and representatives from the medical staff of the Eden Township Hospital District, plans were made to insure the re-election of the three incumbents who will be running again in November 1990. The memorandum which I requested and then purchased from the district, is a xeroxed copy of a summary of discussions held during a retreat held in February 1990 by the board. I have enclosed a copy which refers to my concern.

Eden Township Hospital District is a not-for-profit district hospital which, as I understand it, does not pay taxes and does receive some monies from county and state agencies. It has an elected board. I believe these descriptors indicate that the Board of Directors and staff come under the provisions of Proposition 73 which restricts the use of district staff time and materials to assist in the re-election of incumbents. As you will see from the retreat report, plans were made for hospital staff members to write speeches, publicize and influence the public in behalf of the re-election of the three incumbents. Wouldn't this be a violation of Prop. 73?

I also have a copy of a "Report to the Community" for 1989 which was mass mailed and it contains a picture of the five board members. As I understand it, this is also in violation of Prop. 73. The hospital Foundation also mails out a newsletter and I noticed that pictures of board members are also used in this publication. I am not sure if the Foundation publication falls under the same rules.

I am planning to run for a seat on the hospital board of directors in November 1990. I have filed, this date, FPPC form 501. It would appear from the above, that the incumbents have an unfair and possibly illegal advantage if they can use hospital staff time and materials to enhance their campaigns.

At this time, I am not filing a complaint. This is a request for information and "enlightenment." If you have any questions, please call me (415) 887-0152 X209 (work) or (415) 538-9678 (home).

Sincerely,

Suzanne Barba

Suzanne Barba

enclosure

EHMC Retreat Feb 9-10, 1990

4

8. Much of the retreat concerned itself with the composition status and issues of
the Board, the Medical Staff, and the Administration, culminating with the
three groups making recommendations for improvements, as follows:

Recommendations made by the Board:

a. Push for continuity of the Board through the re-election of incumbents:
   • news releases prior to filing date.
   • stress the positive state of the Hospital
   • we could expand the Board from 5 to 7 members, but this would not
     necessarily make the Board better or provide for more continuity.

b. Ask Medical Staff for support (not financially)—coffees, etc.

c. Public Relations Dept. could help Board members prepare speeches for
   delivery at service club luncheons, etc. (a la Speakers Bureau).

d. Look to the Foundation and subsidiary corporations for future Board
   members—assess the potential.

e. We endorse the policy of providing more services to physicians—an
   Office of Physician Services.

f. Invite more physicians or groups of physicians to help advise the Board
   Planning Committee or to participate in meaningful dialogue and to
   provide input.

Recommendations made by Medical Staff

a. Have regular meetings of current and past leadership of Medical Staff to
   discuss general and specific issues on a regular basis, including physician-
   recruiting, long-range planning. Start with an informal group and then
   organize more formally, if indicated. (This would be for Medical Staff,
   primarily—no Board or Administration representation.)

b. There should be free and easy access of communication between
   physicians/administrators, administrators/Board, and Board/physicians.

c. Services desired for physicians:
   • PR services, under aegis of administration.
   • new Product Lines that provide physician contentment.
   • re Office of Physician Services—set up a meeting between
     administration and Medical Staff to plan its description and
     personnel.

Administration                                                                                                                10

1. Some changes have been made; some are pending.

2. There may be some new Product Lines and departments.

3. There will likely be some changes in the span-of-control and some shifts in
   responsibilities.

4. When the development of Geriatric Services is complete, there may be the need for
   another person to manage it.

5. There may be a Vice President for Continuing Care.


B. RESULTS OF "OVERNIGHT SURVEY"
SUGGESTIONS FOR IMPROVEMENTS IN COMPOSITION

Suggestions for Improving Board of Directors

1. Develop a plan for the re-election of incumbents:
   • increase visibility through media events and hospital publications.
   • encourage/educate others to vote:
     Foundation          public exposure
     patients            Rotary, Lions, etc.
     employees
     physicians
     clients             auxiliary
   • emphasize community/political exposure in advance of elections.
   • emphasize "teamwork" nature of this Board.

2. Continue to communicate openly with Administration and Medical Staff.

3. Foster more of a fiduciary relationship with Medical Staff—better recognition/perks of
   Medical Staff who donate their time.

4. Create an environment for working with a larger number of physicians, around Strategic
   Planning.

5. Assure that possible "heart string" issues are in order.

6. Assure that Board members remain cohesive.

7. More communications with all of our publics so that they know the status of key issues.

Suggestions for Improving Medical Staff

1. Improve the prestige of membership at EHMC.

2. Conduct strategic and long-range planning:
   • for future Medical Staff leadership.
   • for directions for EHMC.
   • for services to be added or enhanced.
   • for specialties that should be attractive to Medical Staff.

12
7. "Perks to physicians" should be looked on as a positive step forward rather than as a
"reluctant favor to a spoiled kid"—reward those who support the Hospital.

8. There is a definite need for a more-balanced work load for each VP—need additional
VPs.

9. Administration should seek feedback from Board and Medical Staff regarding adequate
levels of executive management support.

Suggestions for Improving Medical Staff/Administration Relationships

1. Clarify current Administration chain-of-command for Medical Staff—how to access the
person you need.

2. Assign an administrator to a key member of the Medical Staff or to a new member, to:
• meet physician needs.
• help solve problems.
• advocate for the physician.
• serve as liaison for all parts of the hospital.
• minimize the "red tape" to be dealt with.

3. Hospital contracts vs. physician participation—need contract coordination, joint
contracting ability.

4. Administration should develop a formal program of physician services/relations, with
Medical Staff direction:
• staff retention/recruitment/satisfaction/problemsolving.
• development of Medical Staff leadership.

5. Form a Physician Services Department (or Office):
• enhance retention.
• medical practice-enhancement services.
• problem solving.
• using the hospital appropriately.
• free lunches; preferential parking; free or discounted lab/X-ray services for
physicians and families.
• dispense information to physicians (newsletters, conferences).
• management services.
• computer services.
• billing services.
• joint ventures.
• hospital/technology links.
• help with PPO/HMO contracts.
• malpractice relief.
• keep EHMC technologically and physically current.
• office space at reasonable rates, rent subsidies.

6. Work together to re-elect Board incumbents:
• getting endorsements.
• letters to the editors.
• hold coffees for them.
• make calls to voters.
• persuade Johan Kiehs and Bill Lockyer to support them.

13

Suggestions for Improving Medical Staff/Board Relationships

1. Move medical office suites from District ownership to Development Corporation's
ownership, because of legal limitations on the District. This would permit rent
abatement/deferral as a way of luring/keeping physicians.

2. Use the Development Corporation to provide Medical Staff educational conferences
and/or office support (also, for Board members).

Suggestions for Improving Board/Administration Relationships

1. Provide assistance to Board members in the writing or wording of their campaign
statements.

2. Develop a program to nurture/educate potential new Board members.

Suggestions in General

1. The Foundation should foster a structure for physician recognition.

Suggestion for new position—Physician Liaison Secretary

1. Works in Medical Staff Office.

2. Officially greets new staff on behalf of the Hospital, Medical Staff, and Board.

3. Answers complaints; offers to solve, or properly direct, problems.

4. Facilitates new physicians' usage of the Hospital to best advantage.

5. Acts as a sounding board. Needs to have a proper personality (gracious, smiles, tactful,
friendly).

6. Informs the physicians of ongoing services available to his/her good potential.

7. Serves as "matchmaker" to start a "big brother" program—introduces veteran physicians
to new physicians (may not be in the same specialties).

C. RECOMMENDATIONS MADE BY BOARD,
MEDICAL STAFF, AND ADMINISTRATION
FOR IMPROVING COMPOSITION AND RELATIONSHIPS

(See Executive Summary for recommendations made.)

sections.question: Wouldn't this be a violation of Prop. 73? I also have a copy of a "Report to the Community" for 1989 which was mass mailed and it contains a picture of the five board members. As I understand it, this is also in violation of Prop. 73. The hospital Foundation also mails out a newsletter and I noticed that pictures of board members are also used in this publication. I am not sure if the Foundation publication falls under the same rules.
sections.question_synthetic: The sender asks whether Eden Township Hospital District's use of district staff time and materials to assist in re-electing incumbent board members, and the district's mass mailing of materials featuring incumbent board members, violates Proposition 73's restrictions on public agency resources being used for political campaigns.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes two main parts: (1) the inquiry letter from Suzanne Barba dated June 15, 1990, and (2) attached retreat summary documents from February 1990. No FPPC conclusion/advice section exists in this document. The sender explicitly states 'At this time, I am not filing a complaint. This is a request for information and enlightenment.' High confidence in question extraction; lower confidence due to lack of formal section headers and presence of attached materials that provide context.

I am not sure if the Foundation publication falls under the same rules.

embedding.summary: A prospective board candidate for Eden Township Hospital District requests FPPC guidance on whether the district violated Proposition 73 by using staff time and materials during a February 1990 board retreat to plan the re-election of three incumbent board members, and by featuring board members in a mass-mailed community report and foundation newsletter.
